provenance.source_corpus: Sphagnum moss moisture retention following the re‐vegetation of degraded peatlands

source_spans.text: In the autumn of 1999, ecosystem scale restoration commenced using the standard Canadian restoration technique
Sphagnum rubellum was present at the restored site

Lower samples for the natural and naturally re-vegetated sites had a bulk density of 43 š 5 and 41 š 11 kg m /NUL3, respectively, almost twice as high as the value for lower samples from the restored site (24 š 4k gm /NUL3)

Residual moisture content at a soil water pressure of /NUL200 mb (/DC2r) was significantly lower ( p< 0Ð05) for the restored site in comparison to the natural and naturally re-vegetated sites for the lower samples
Porosity was significantly different among the three sites for upper as well as lower samples... higher porosity of the lower samples from the restored site
Capitula density at the surface of the new moss layer was lowest for the natural site, and highest for the restored site
